title: WAD Management with YAWM
requirements: An SD card or USB drive, FAT32 formatted
warnings: Only install WADs from sources you trust. A bad or mismatched WAD (like the wrong IOS version) can cause problems on your console.
_note: YAWM ModMii Edition is a straightforward WAD manager — it installs WAD files (channels, IOS, forwarders) and can launch homebrew apps too.

steps.title: Before you start
steps.body:
**YAWM ModMii Edition** installs `.wad` files onto your Wii — useful for channel forwarders, IOS installs, or any homebrew distributed as a WAD instead of a `.dol`/`.elf`.

First, I'll check your SD card or USB drive is FAT32.

steps.title: Put YAWM on your storage device
steps.body:
Graphite downloads **YAWM ModMii Edition** and unpacks it into the `apps` folder on your SD card or USB drive.

If you already have WAD files you want to install, drop them into a `wad` folder at the root of the same storage device now — YAWM automatically opens that folder if it exists.

steps.title: Clean up and eject the card
steps.body:
macOS scatters hidden junk files (`.DS_Store`, `._` files) onto SD cards and USB drives, and Wii homebrew can choke on them.

Press the button, and Graphite strips those files off the card and **safely ejects it for you**. Once it pops off, pull it out.

steps.title: Install a WAD
steps.body:
Plug the storage device into your Wii and power it on. Launch the **Homebrew Channel** and open **YAWM**.

1. Select your storage device as the source.
2. Browse to your WAD file(s) — YAWM opens the `wad` folder automatically if present.
3. Press **A** on a WAD to select it, or press **+** to queue several at once.
4. Press **A** again to start installing.

Wait for each install to finish before removing the storage device.

steps.title: You're done
steps.body: Your WAD(s) are installed as channels (or updated IOS) on your Wii. Re-run this guide any time you need to install more.
